checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any fabricated paragraphs or sentences. All text present in the output, including headings and list items, has a direct basis in the source document. No invented table-of-contents or hallucinated body text was identified.

The conference bridge phone number (888-808-6929) and ID (8153047#) in the output match the source exactly. No fabricated emails, URLs, or addresses were found. Note that one existing link was omitted (evaluated under C6), but no fake information was added.

Both pages of the document are represented in the output. While several sections are severely truncated (missing sub-points and financial data), the sections themselves (e.g., 'Consent Agenda', 'Executive Director Report') are present in the structure. Truncation is evaluated under C5.

The primary table (Tab/Topic/Presenter) is accurately captured. The financial data in the source is presented as indented text blocks rather than formal tables with borders; the output attempts to represent these as list items but fails to capture the data rows. This content loss is more accurately categorized as a text omission within a section (C5) rather than a row count error in a formal table structure.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis:
There are major text omissions in three key areas: 
1. Item 4 (Consent Agenda): The detailed financial figures for the NG 911 Fund, CMRS Fund, PSAP Fund, and Grant Fund (including balances and disbursements for November/December 2018) are completely missing.
2. Item 4g (Peer Reviewer Approvals): The five sub-items (i. Vicki Callicutt to v. Tracy Trogdon) are missing.
3. Item 5 (Executive Director Report): The six sub-items (a. New Board Member Orientation to f. Reminder of Nominations) are entirely absent. 
These omissions constitute a loss of approximately 30-40% of the document's total meaningful information, far exceeding the 25% threshold for a MAJOR defect.
checks.b_evidence_source: November 2018 Fund Balance $27,867,858 ... December Grant Funds Encumbered -$29,930,421 [and] i. Vicki Callicutt ... v. Tracy Trogdon [and] a) New Board Member Orientation ... f) Reminder of Nominations for 911 Board Vice Chair
checks.b_evidence_output: b) NG 911 Fund ... c) CMRS Fund ... d) PSAP Fund ... e) Grant Fund [and] g) Peer Reviewer Approvals – Presented as Recommendation of Standards Committee [and] 5. Executive Director Report

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The header section in the output omits the specific link text '20190111 NC 911 Board WebEx Link' which appears in red in the source PDF. This is a specific omission of meeting access information.
checks.b_evidence_output: NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING\nFriday, January 18, 2019\nWebEx and Conference Bridge

checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain duplicated content from the source PDF. Document headers and footers that appear on both pages of the source are correctly excluded from the main section content to avoid repetition.
The heading hierarchy is logical and follows a standard pattern (H1 for title, H2 for major sections, H3 for sub-sections). There are no skipped levels or logical inconsistencies in the nesting.

The table structure is severely corrupted. The source document uses a 3-column table (Tab, Topic, Presenter) that spans two pages. The output truncates this table after Item 4. Furthermore, the column alignment within the captured rows is inconsistent; for example, the first two data rows ('Call to Order' and 'Roll Call') contain only 2 cells, while subsequent rows contain 3. The structural association between topics and presenters for the second half of the agenda is completely lost.
checks.b_evidence_source: A 3-column table (Tab, Topic, Presenter) starting on page 1 and continuing to item 13 on page 2.
checks.b_evidence_output: The table element (reading_order 5) ends after item 4. Items 5-13 are converted into lists and loose paragraphs.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order for the second page diverges significantly from the source's intent. In the source, each topic is immediately followed by its presenter in a horizontal layout. In the output, a list of topics (items 6-13) is presented first, followed by a series of separate paragraphs containing the presenter names. This makes the document nearly impossible to navigate meaningfully for a screen reader user as the contextual connection between item and presenter is severed.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2 table layout aligning presenters (e.g., Mike Reitz, David Bone) horizontally with their topics (e.g., Education Committee Report, Funding Committee Report).
checks.b_evidence_output: A single list (reading_order 13) containing all topics, followed by a block of individual paragraphs (reading_order 14-20) containing the names.

checks.a_analysis: The list structure fails to maintain proper nesting and completeness. Sub-items 'a' through 'f' under Item 4 are flattened and separated from their parent item. More critically, the nested list of sub-items 'a' through 'f' belonging to Item 5 (Executive Director Report) is entirely omitted from the structure, though it is clearly present in the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: Item 5 includes sub-items a) New Board Member Orientation through f) Reminder of Nominations...
checks.b_evidence_output: The list item '5. Executive Director Report' (reading_order 11) has no nested content or following elements representing these sub-items.

checks.a_analysis: The output fails to capture a primary hyperlink provided in the source header. The '20190111 NC 911 Board WebEx Link' text and its underlying URL are entirely missing from the document structure.
checks.b_evidence_output: Text and link are missing from the heading area (reading_order 2).

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the agenda is significantly altered in the HTML output. In the source PDF, agenda items 5-12 are presented in a table format that visually links each topic to its presenter (e.g., '6. Education Committee Report' is horizontally aligned with 'Mike Reitz'). In the HTML output, these items are rendered as a bulleted list, and the presenters' names are detached and listed in a separate block of text far below the list of topics. This breaks the visual flow and the meaningful association between the data. Additionally, the logo, which appears at the top left of the PDF, has been relocated to the middle of the document in the HTML.
checks.b_evidence_source: In the source PDF on page 2, agenda items 5 through 12 are aligned with their respective presenters in columns (e.g., Item 6 is aligned with Mike Reitz).
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML screenshot, items 5-13 are shown as a bulleted list, followed by a separate list of names ('Mike Reitz', 'David Bone', etc.) that are no longer visually associated with their specific agenda items.

checks.a_analysis: The table structure for the agenda is partially lost; while items 1-4 are in a table, the continuation of the agenda (items 5-13) is rendered as a list, breaking the row/column relationship for presenters. More critically, the structured financial data within section 4 (sub-items b through e) is completely missing. In the PDF, these items contain multiple rows of dates, fund balances, and disbursement figures. In the HTML rendering, only the header for each fund is visible (e.g., 'b) NG 911 Fund'), while all the underlying numerical data is absent. This renders the table-like content unreadable and incomplete.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 1, Section 4, sub-items b) through e) contain multiple lines of financial data such as 'November 2018 Fund Balance $27,867,858'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot for section 4 shows the bullet points 'b) NG 911 Fund' through 'e) Grant Fund' with no associated financial figures or sub-lines.
checks.b_evidence_location: Section 4, sub-items b, c, d, and e

checks.a_analysis: The meaningful 'NC DIT 911 Board' logo from the header of the source PDF is present in the HTML rendering, although its position has shifted and it is rendered with a black background instead of blue/grey.

No body content (paragraphs, tables, or sections) is visually duplicated. The appearance of section names in the sidebar Table of Contents and as main headers in the body is expected accessible navigation design.
